programmes.show_description: A woman claims her ex-boyfriend slashed her tyres but fails to prove a word of her story; he is still in the hot seat for alleged theft of her property. S24, Ep30
Sad news is delivered in the midst of a fight between ex-roommates over ruined credit and alleged identity theft. S24, Ep31
A legally-blind woman is court ordered to take anger management classes after slapping her husband; she sues the class owner for unpaid wages. S24, Ep32
A man claims he was attacked in a sober-living facility; his housemate says he was leaving the stove on overnight to heat the place. S22, Ep165
Two women with children by the same man go after each other in a fight over defamation and loss of income; a third potential baby mama comes into play. S22, Ep166
A single father claims he had no choice but to take heroic efforts to protect his home from Hurricane Irma and used public property to do so. S22, Ep167
The Judge reads a woman the riot act when she allegedly uses the family court system to evict a tenant by means of a questionable restraining order. S22, Ep168
When a German Shepherd puppy dies, pet owners blame the breeder; the Judge investigates allegations of parvo running rampant in the litter. S22, Ep169
A man denies having intimate relations with a woman; she claims he lived with her for two months and assaulted her daughter. S22, Ep170
A woman goes after her estranged daughter-in-law for thousands in unpaid loans; she is met with rage over an allegedly deadbeat husband. S22, Ep171
An unexpected visitor flies into the courtroom during a case between ex-lovers fighting over a kicked-down door and an alleged lock-out. S16, Ep58
Art Museum Director Barry Horn and drug dealer Ivan Martinez become friends. Within six months, Barry is dead. Ivan admits to the killing, saying Barry sexually abused him. S4, Ep5
A person on the trip of a lifetime is lost in the Australian bush without food and water. S3, Ep5
A pedestrian is involved in an accident when a bus shunts a parked car into him. S3, Ep7
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
All hell breaks loose when a woman's handyman starts dating one of her tenants; she files a restraining order and sues him for alleged vandalism. S22, Ep137
After a young man is rescued by a family friend, he makes a deal to move in and pay her back; she says he was like a son but sues him anyway. S22, Ep138
An evening of tequila drinking evolves into a reckless car ride that ends with an alleged collision. S22, Ep139
Squirrels in an attic, trash in the basement and tenant identity confusion come into play when a woman sues her landlord. S22, Ep140
A woman who claims her ex-fiancé wasn't ready to get married sues him for alleged loans to buy a motorcycle, make a mortgage payment, and pay his tuition. S16, Ep18
A man is accused of making homophobic comments and being rude to a fellow roommate; he sues his ex-roommate for the return of rent and a security deposit. S16, Ep19
A $5,000 marijuana drug deal gone wrong is disputed by ex-roommates who fight over alleged damages as a result of a police raid. S16, Ep20
After eight weeks of work, a young man says he was injured on the job and sues his boss for $5,000 for an alleged wrongful termination and a workman's comp claim. S16, Ep21
After being incarcerated for a year, a man comes home to find that his car has vanished; he thinks it's been repossessed, but an ex-friend claims it is rightfully hers. S24, Ep33
A woman hires a contractor to convert an older vehicle into her new store; she sues him for allegedly substandard work and leaky, moldy windows. S24, Ep34
A woman with 15 dogs allegedly reneges on her promise to breed a puppy with a champion blood line and instead decides to spay the animal. S24, Ep35
The Judge advises a mother to spend more time on a custody battle than a fight over an alleged small loan to the grandmother of her children. S22, Ep172
A woman sues her adoptive mother for alleged damages to her home and claims she vandalised her car as well; a neighbour steps up to testify. S22, Ep173
When a young man's living room space is invaded by a roommate's relatives, the police get involved. S22, Ep174
A teenager accused of stealing thousands from his grandmother's safety deposit box, says his grandfather gave him the lock combination. S22, Ep175
A model's emotional-support French Bulldog is allegedly attacked by a German Shepherd Husky at a fashion shoot. S22, Ep176
A woman claims she never hit a fellow motorist's vehicle but offered to get out of her Tesla and personally microfiber any scratches from it. S22, Ep177
After a mother witnesses her son's violent death, she and her husband struggle to payback alleged loans to a family member. S22, Ep178
A little boy bursts into tears after taking the stand; he accuses another child of throwing a rock and hitting him in the eye. His mother sues for damages. S16, Ep34
A recovering drug addict allegedly gets hooked on opiates again after moving into an illegal apartment in a home where two teens live. He is sued for back rent and medical expenses. S16, Ep35
Smashing a wine glass over a man's head and causing thousands of dollars in medical bills is the accusation laid against a woman; she claims he was aggressively hitting on her. S16, Ep36
A fire causes property damage leaving a man and his girlfriend to sue their former landlord for damages and the return of their security deposit. S16, Ep37
Nurse Marie Whiston claimed her husband died naturally while in her care. An autopsy revealed lethal insulin - she used her medical expertise to murder him. S3, Ep1
A hoaxer is faking accidents for fun; and the UK's biggest Blue Badge fraud is unearthed. S2, Ep4
Prolific serial killer, Gerard Schaefer, operated under the security of his police badge. Psychological Criminologist, Dr Bryanna Fox, investigates the red flags. S1, Ep1
Nurse Beverley Allitt murdered four children and harmed nine more in Lincolnshire, abusing trust until a pattern of deaths exposed her. S1, Ep4
Esther Gonzalez was killed in 1979 while walking in Riverside. Randy Williamson, cleared by polygraph, lived freely until 2014. In 2023, forensic genealogy closed the cold case. S6, Ep3
After being incarcerated for a year, a man comes home to find that his car has vanished; he thinks it's been repossessed, but an ex-friend claims it is rightfully hers. S24, Ep33
A woman hires a contractor to convert an older vehicle into her new store; she sues him for allegedly substandard work and leaky, moldy windows. S24, Ep34
A woman with 15 dogs allegedly reneges on her promise to breed a puppy with a champion blood line and instead decides to spay the animal. S24, Ep35
The Judge advises a mother to spend more time on a custody battle than a fight over an alleged small loan to the grandmother of her children. S22, Ep172
A woman sues her adoptive mother for alleged damages to her home and claims she vandalised her car as well; a neighbour steps up to testify. S22, Ep173
When a young man's living room space is invaded by a roommate's relatives, the police get involved. S22, Ep174
A teenager accused of stealing thousands from his grandmother's safety deposit box, says his grandfather gave him the lock combination. S22, Ep175
A model's emotional-support French Bulldog is allegedly attacked by a German Shepherd Husky at a fashion shoot. S22, Ep176
A woman claims she never hit a fellow motorist's vehicle but offered to get out of her Tesla and personally microfiber any scratches from it. S22, Ep177
After a mother witnesses her son's violent death, she and her husband struggle to payback alleged loans to a family member. S22, Ep178
A woman regrets co-signing for her live-in boyfriend's Harley Davidson; she sues him for allegedly ruining her credit and for the return of motorcycle gear. S16, Ep59
Charles Dean Hood's story is one of strip clubs, drug deals and judicial corruption. Convicted of double murder, he spent 24 years on Death Row pleading his innocence. S4, Ep6
A building site worker digs up more than he bargained for when he finds a World War II bomb. S3, Ep8
Fire crews battle to reach a woman trapped in her flat. S3, Ep9
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
A man returns home to find his beloved Dachshund maimed and bleeding in a neighbour's backyard. S22, Ep154
A landlord accuses his teen tenant's boyfriend of head-butting him during an altercation; when he pushes back, the police are called. S22, Ep155
Concerned about the fate of a Pomeranian puppy, a breeder drives 6 hours to rescue the animal from an allegedly negligent pet owner. S22, Ep156
A woman being sued by her ex-lover for the return of his belongings claims she had to sell them to survive. S22, Ep157
A 13-year-old boy is asked to step out of the courtroom before his family members start fighting over living expenses and a restraining order. S16, Ep22
Breaking up via a text message, inappropriate courtroom attire and a used computer come into play when exes dispute custody of a Lenovo laptop. S16, Ep23
After dating a single parent for only eight weeks, a woman claims she loaned him money to buy a new truck because his old truck was in danger of being repossessed. S16, Ep24
A woman slides into a fire hydrant during an ice storm and totals the car her father's friend was letting her borrow; she admits it was the second car she has totalled. S16, Ep25
A woman drops off her two children at daycare and doesn't pick them up for months; she is sued for childcare costs. S24, Ep36
The owner of a cleaning company goes after a former employee who denies stealing clients from him. S24, Ep37
A man trying to recover from a DUI charge regrets putting his car's title into his girlfriend's name; she demands payment for driving him around. S24, Ep38
A girl suffers a fractured clavicle after colliding with a moving vehicle; the driver sues for damages to her car and refuses to answer the Judge's pointed question. S22, Ep179
After allegedly destroying property in a fit of rage, a woman breaks back into her former home; her ex-boyfriend wants his dog back. S22, Ep180
When the price of a ring to be designed with a family heirloom goes up by thousands, a woman sues a jeweller for the return of her deposit. S22, Ep181
An American Bully dog allegedly comes flying out of his home and attacks a man and his toy poodle; multiple deep arm bites and vet bills come into play. S22, Ep182
When a woman sells her daughter a used car for a 25% profit and then repossesses it, her daughter reports it stolen and gets the police involved. S22, Ep183
An allegedly less than desirable tenant accuses her landlord of ignoring a rat infestation and stopping her mail. S22, Ep184
An unsuspecting man buying a car for his son never imagined that the car he was purchasing had a 3k title loan on it. S22, Ep185
A single mother demands that her ex-lover pay her back for alleged loans to pay other outstanding loans and utility bills. S16, Ep38
A mother is accused of treating her adult daughter like a baby when she stands up for her in court. Her daughter claims she was conned into paying more rent than was fair. S16, Ep39
Nine stitches are required after a man is hit in the head with a guitar; video evidence is submitted which shows the on-stage accident. S16, Ep40
Racial profiling during a routine traffic stop is the accusation laid against a veteran California cop who sues motorists for filling an allegedly false complaint against him. S16, Ep41
Katie Wilding sought romance but found abuse. Her death was ruled as an overdose, but her family wanted justice. S3, Ep2
A dentist fleeces the NHS; and a con artist makes thousands pretending to know Hilary Clinton. S2, Ep5
Danny Rolling was a victim of abuse but why did he choose to commit murder? Join Dr Bryanna Fox as she investigates Rolling's red flags. S1, Ep2
Marie Buls was an elderly woman in the care of wealthy spinster Noreen O'Connor. As time progressed O'Connor became convinced that Marie was possessed by the devil. S7, Ep4
In 1991, 16-year-old Sarah Yarborough was murdered outside her school. Eyewitnesses saw a man - but it took nearly 30 years and forensic genealogy to find him. S6, Ep4
